title: Determined Planning | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa | SB 4.4.2 | 2019-07-19
speaker: HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

segments.text: The word Aprati Purusham used in this verse means one who has no equal.
Lord Shiva has no equal in the material world in regard to equality towards everyone.
His wife Sati knew that her husband was equal towards everyone.
So why in this case was he so unkind to his wife that he did not allow her to go to her father's house?
This distressed her more than she could tolerate and she looked at her husband as if she were ready to blast him with her vision.
In other words, since Lord Shiva is the Atma, Shiva also means Atma,
it is indicated here that Sati was prepared to commit suicide.
Another meaning of the word Aprati Purusha is the personality who has no rival.
Since Lord Shiva could not be persuaded to give her permission,
Sati took shelter of a woman's last weapon, weeping,
which forces a husband to agree to the proposal of his wife.
That is the sense of the Bhaktivedanta Parva.
So a man may invent a powerful weapon like this.
This is a very powerful weapon.
But back at home, the wife is having a much more powerful weapon called weeping,
which will force a husband to agree to the proposal.
So here the whole issue is the proposal.
Sati has a proposal to her great husband.
And Lord Shiva has not accepted that proposal.
She wants to see her relatives, that is the desire.
Now once the desires are prevented,
we all know the result will be Rusha, anger.
it means that some proposal or some desires of ours are Pratighatha, prevented.
what is that desire or proposal that I have made,

then Durmana, she started feeling sorry in the mind.
